title: PDF Scanner: Replace Live Camera with Choose from Files + Auto Edge Detection & Full Scan-to-PDF Workflow

requirements.summary: Replace 'Live Scanner' camera button with 'Choose from Files' button that opens native file picker accepting images (JPG, PNG, JPEG, WEBP) and PDFs, importing them as scanned page entries
acceptanceCriteria: No 'Live Scanner' or camera button is visible in the PDF Scanner panel.
A 'Choose from Files' button is prominently displayed; on mobile it reads 'Choose from Files (Google Files / Scanner)'.
Clicking the button opens a native file picker accepting image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp, and application/pdf MIME types.
Selected image files are immediately appended to the scanned pages list as individual entries.
Selected PDF files have each page rendered via PDF.js and appended as individual scanned page entries.
No navigator.mediaDevices or getUserMedia calls remain in the component.
All imported pages appear as numbered thumbnails in the scanned pages list panel.

file_operations.description: Remove all camera-related code (camera/useCamera imports, video elements, camera state, getUserMedia calls). Replace the 'Live Scanner' button with a 'Choose from Files' button (mobile label: 'Choose from Files (Google Files / Scanner)'). Implement a file input element accepting 'image/png,image/jpeg,image/jpg,image/webp,application/pdf'. On file selection, process image files as scanned page entries and use PDF.js (loaded from CDN following the pattern in frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/usePDFOperations.ts) to render each page of selected PDFs as individual scanned page entries. Ensure all imported pages are added to the scannedPages state array with unique IDs, original image data URLs, and metadata.

requirements.summary: Implement automatic document edge detection and perspective crop for each imported image using Canvas-based pixel contrast analysis, with a Manual Crop fallback mode featuring draggable corner handles
acceptanceCriteria: After a file is imported, the component attempts automatic document edge detection on the Canvas.
A perspective-corrected crop is applied automatically to each imported image and shown as the default preview.
A toggle or radio control allows switching between Auto Crop and Manual Crop modes.
In Manual Crop mode, an interactive overlay with draggable corner handles is shown on the image.
An 'Apply Crop' button confirms the crop and updates the scanned page entry.
A 'Reset' button reverts the page to the uncropped original image.
If auto-detection fails or finds no clear edges, the full image is used as the default without crashing.

file_operations.description: Implement automatic edge detection logic using Canvas-based pixel contrast analysis to detect document quadrilaterals. After each image import, run edge detection and apply a perspective transform crop automatically. Store both the original uncropped image and the auto-cropped result in each scanned page entry. Add crop mode state (auto/manual) with a toggle control in the UI. Implement a Manual Crop mode with an SVG or div overlay containing four draggable corner handles positioned over the currently selected page preview. Provide 'Apply Crop' and 'Reset' buttons; Apply Crop updates the page's cropped image, Reset restores the original uncropped image. If edge detection finds no clear quadrilateral, default to showing the full uncropped image without errors.

requirements.summary: Add per-page image adjustment controls (Brightness slider -100 to +100, Contrast slider -100 to +100, 'Darken Text' toggle) with real-time Canvas preview updates
acceptanceCriteria: Each scanned page has its own independent Brightness slider (range -100 to +100).
Each scanned page has its own independent Contrast slider (range -100 to +100).
A 'Darken Text' toggle is present for each page; enabling it applies a Canvas threshold/levels filter that darkens text and lightens the background.
All adjustments update the Canvas preview in real time when sliders or the toggle are changed.
Adjustments are stored per page so switching between pages in the list shows each page's own settings.
Adjustment settings are applied when generating the final PDF output.

file_operations.description: Add per-page adjustment state fields (brightness: number, contrast: number, darkenText: boolean) to each scanned page entry. Render a Brightness slider (range -100 to +100, default 0), a Contrast slider (range -100 to +100, default 0), and a 'Darken Text' checkbox for the currently selected page. When any adjustment control changes, update the corresponding page's state and re-render the Canvas preview in real time by applying brightness/contrast transformations and a threshold filter (if darkenText is enabled) using Canvas ImageData pixel manipulation. Ensure each page retains its independent adjustment settings when switching between pages in the scanned pages list.

requirements.summary: Display scanned pages list panel with numbered thumbnails and remove (✕) buttons, allowing users to append additional pages at any time
acceptanceCriteria: The scanned pages list panel displays a thumbnail and page number for each imported page.
Each thumbnail has a visible ✕ remove button; clicking it removes that page from the list.
The user can click 'Choose from Files' again at any time to append more pages to the existing list.
The list updates immediately when pages are added or removed.
Page numbers update correctly after removal.

file_operations.description: Render a scanned pages list panel (glass-card surface) that maps over the scannedPages array. For each page, display a thumbnail canvas or image preview showing the cropped/adjusted result, a page number label (1-indexed), and a remove button (✕) that filters out that page from the scannedPages array on click. Ensure the 'Choose from Files' button can be clicked multiple times to append additional pages to the existing scannedPages array. Update page numbers dynamically whenever pages are added or removed.

requirements.summary: Implement 'Convert to PDF' button that generates a single PDF from all scanned pages using pdf-lib (loaded from CDN), embedding each page with applied crop and adjustments, and provides a 'Download PDF' button
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Convert to PDF' button is visible when at least one scanned page exists in the list.
Clicking it applies each page's crop and adjustment settings to the Canvas, then embeds the result as a full-page image in the output PDF using pdf-lib.
A loading/progress indicator is shown during PDF generation.
After generation, a 'Download PDF' button appears and triggers a browser file download of the resulting PDF.
The generated PDF contains all non-removed pages in their listed order.
No errors are thrown if a page's canvas or image data is missing; such pages are skipped with a warning.

file_operations.description: Add a 'Convert to PDF' button that is enabled when scannedPages.length > 0. On click, show a loading indicator and iterate over all scanned pages. For each page, render its cropped image to a Canvas, apply the page's brightness, contrast, and darkenText adjustments using Canvas pixel manipulation, then convert the Canvas to a PNG data URL. Load pdf-lib from CDN using the same dynamic import pattern as frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/usePDFOperations.ts. Create a new PDFDocument, and for each processed page data URL, embed the PNG image and add a full-page entry to the PDF. After all pages are embedded, serialize the PDF to bytes and store the result. Hide the loading indicator and display a 'Download PDF' button. On 'Download PDF' click, trigger a browser download of the PDF bytes with the user-specified filename. If a page's data is missing or errors occur during processing, log a warning and skip that page without throwing.

requirements.summary: Add a filename input field pre-filled with 'scanned-document.pdf' that validates and automatically appends '.pdf' extension if omitted
acceptanceCriteria: A filename input field is visible in the export/download area of the PDF Scanner panel.
The input is pre-filled with 'scanned-document.pdf' by default.
The downloaded file uses the user-specified filename.
If the filename is empty, downloading is blocked or the default name is used.
If the user enters a name without '.pdf', the extension is appended automatically before download.

file_operations.description: Add a filename state field initialized to 'scanned-document.pdf'. Render a text input field in the export controls area (glass-card surface) bound to the filename state. When the 'Download PDF' button is clicked, validate that the filename is non-empty (if empty, use the default 'scanned-document.pdf'). If the filename does not end with '.pdf', append '.pdf' automatically. Use the validated filename when triggering the browser download of the generated PDF.

requirements.summary: Apply dark-first glassmorphism design system to all PDF Scanner UI elements using glass-card surfaces, tool-btn classes, teal/cyan accents, and ensure mobile-responsive vertical stacking
acceptanceCriteria: All panels in the PDF Scanner use the glass-card CSS class for surfaces.
All action buttons use the tool-btn CSS class.
Teal/cyan accent colors are applied to primary action buttons and active states.
On mobile screens, all panels stack vertically without horizontal overflow.
The design is visually consistent with the other PDF Converter tabs and the rest of the application.

file_operations.description: Apply the glass-card CSS class to all container panels (file import area, scanned pages list, adjustment controls, export controls). Apply the tool-btn CSS class to all action buttons ('Choose from Files', 'Apply Crop', 'Reset', 'Convert to PDF', 'Download PDF'). Use teal/cyan accent classes (from frontend/src/index.css) for primary action buttons and active/selected states. Structure the layout to stack vertically on small screens using responsive Tailwind utilities (e.g., flex flex-col gap-4 on mobile, optionally grid or flex-row on larger screens). Ensure no horizontal overflow occurs on mobile viewports.
